pattern: DFS from the root; each subtree that contains at least one apple contributes 2 to the total (one step down, one step back).
hints: Model the undirected tree as an adjacency list rooted at node 0; a DFS naturally computes the minimum walk.
For each node, recurse into its children; if a child's subtree contains an apple (or the child itself has one), you must travel there and back — that costs 2 steps.
Sum up the 2-step costs from every subtree that is non-empty of apples; the root contributes 0 overhead since you start and end there.

edge_inputs.description: only root has apple — no movement needed
apple only in a deep leaf — must traverse entire path and back
edges given in reverse order — undirected graph

stress.description: star graph with 100000 nodes where half the leaves have apples
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = size
    edges = [[0, i] for i in range(1, n)]
    has_apple = [False] + [rng.random() < 0.5 for _ in range(n - 1)]
    return [n, edges, has_apple]
